commit.name: Weather Card
commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

issues.description: The 5-day forecast cards are clickable but there is no selection, drill-in, or other interactive behavior wired to those clicks, so the list presents interaction affordance without a usable action.
issues.fix: The 5-day forecast cards are clickable but there is no selection, drill-in, or other interactive behavior wired to those clicks, so the list presents interaction affordance without a usable action.

issues.description: There are no hover/focus feedback styles or accessible state cues shown for the clickable forecast cards, so users cannot tell they are interactive or navigate them confidently.
issues.fix: There are no hover/focus feedback styles or accessible state cues shown for the clickable forecast cards, so users cannot tell they are interactive or navigate them confidently.

issues.description: Forecast day cards are clickable via Clickable, but no keyboard interaction handlers or explicit role are provided; ensure the cards are rendered as real buttons or support keyboard activation (Enter/Space)
issues.fix: Forecast day cards are clickable via Clickable, but no keyboard interaction handlers or explicit role are provided; ensure the cards are rendered as real buttons or support keyboard activation (Enter/Space)

issues.description: The clickable forecast cards rely on aria-label alone; add visible focus styling and keyboard semantics if they remain interactive
issues.fix: The clickable forecast cards rely on aria-label alone; add visible focus styling and keyboard semantics if they remain interactive

evaluation.critique: The component has a solid structure with proper use of design tokens and responsive layout, but suffers from incomplete code (abruptly cut off in the forecast rendering), missing hover/focus states on interactive elements, and a hardcoded caption 'Feels prominent and current' that should be dynamic. The forecast cards lack proper interactivity polish despite being marked as Clickable.
